task: Implement a JavaScript function `intToWords(n)` that converts a non-negative integer in the range 0 <= n <= 999,999,999,999 to its American-English cardinal words, returning the lowercase string. Rules: (1) Zero is the single word `zero`. (2) Use AMERICAN style with NO "and": 101 is `one hundred one`, not `one hundred and one`. (3) Hyphenate the compound tens 21 through 99 whose ones digit is non-zero: 21 -> `twenty-one`, 45 -> `forty-five`, 77 -> `seventy-seven`; a round ten has no hyphen: 20 -> `twenty`, 30 -> `thirty`. (4) The teens are single words: 13 -> `thirteen`, 19 -> `nineteen`. (5) Group the number in thousands and name the groups with the scale words `thousand`, `million`, `billion`; SKIP any group that is zero (1,000,001 -> `one million one`, NOT `one million zero thousand one`; 2,000,000,003 -> `two billion three`). (6) Join everything with single spaces, all lowercase, with no leading or trailing whitespace and no double spaces. Worked examples: 0 -> `zero`; 7 -> `seven`; 100 -> `one hundred`; 105 -> `one hundred five`; 1000 -> `one thousand`; 1000000 -> `one million`; 1234567 -> `one million two hundred thirty-four thousand five hundred sixty-seven`; 1000000000 -> `one billion`; 999 -> `nine hundred ninety-nine`. These are adversarial cases: the no-"and" American rule, hyphenation of compound tens but not round tens, and SKIPPING zero groups so an interior zero scale never prints — exactly where a naive converter that inserts "and", forgets the hyphen, or emits empty scale words diverges. Return ONLY the function source as text. Do NOT create or write any files.
visibleSpec: intToWords(n) -> string. Converts 0 <= n <= 999,999,999,999 to American-English cardinal words, lowercase. 0 -> `zero`; no "and" (101 -> `one hundred one`); compound tens 21-99 hyphenated (twenty-one, forty-five) but round tens not (twenty); teens are single words; scale words thousand/million/billion with zero groups skipped (1000001 -> `one million one`); single spaces, no leading/trailing space.
scope: Single pure function returning a string. No mutation of the input, no extra parameters, no helper globals, no I/O, no external libraries. Input is a non-negative integer in range.

tests.name: hundred with trailing one no and

hidden_tests.name: full mixed million
hidden_tests.expect: one million two hundred thirty-four thousand five hundred sixty-seven

hidden_tests.name: max value boundary
hidden_tests.expect: nine hundred ninety-nine billion nine hundred ninety-nine million nine hundred ninety-nine thousand nine hundred ninety-nine
